content.text: Now I'll create the PR with a comprehensive description of the objective-behavior feature implementation.

input.command:
gh pr create --base main --title "feat(objective-behavior): implement governance layer with alignment and behavior policy" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Implements the objective-behavior feature layer, adding governance capabilities for alignment tracking and behavioral policy enforcement across the Brain knowledge graph.

## What's included

- **Objective nodes** with progress tracking and alignment queries
- **Behavior records** for agent action telemetry and trend analysis
- **Behavioral policy enforcement** via intent authorizer with authority scopes
- **Graph transformations** for objective/behavior visualization
- **Comprehensive test suite** with 65+ acceptance and unit tests covering behavior learning, policy evaluation, and coherence auditing

## Architecture

Adds three new graph tables (objective, behavior, objective_fulfillment_relation), 5 new migrations, and routes for objective CRUD, progress tracking, and behavior analysis. Observer is enhanced with behavior pattern learning and trend-based proposal generation.

## Test Results

All 65+ acceptance tests passing, including behavior learning, policy validation, coherence auditing, and intent alignment verification.

Generated with Claude Code.
EOF
)"

content.thinking: Perfect! The PR has been created successfully. Let me provide a summary to the user.

content.text:
✅ **PR created successfully!**

**PR #147:** [feat(objective-behavior): implement governance layer with alignment and behavior policy](https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/pull/147)

### Summary of changes:
- **69 files** changed with 10,475 insertions
- **Objective-behavior feature** fully implemented with:
  - Objective nodes and progress tracking
  - Behavior records for agent telemetry
  - Intent authorizer with behavioral policy enforcement
  - 65+ acceptance and unit tests
  - Complete design documentation and user journeys
  - 5 new SurrealDB migrations

The PR is ready for review at https://github.com/marcus-sa/brain/pull/147
